title: Quadratic Equation Solver - Find Roots of ax²+bx+c=0
description: Free quadratic equation solver. Find the roots of any quadratic equation using the quadratic formula. Shows discriminant and step-by-step solution.
keywords: quadratic equation solver, quadratic formula calculator, find roots, ax2+bx+c, discriminant
intro: Solve quadratic equations of the form ax² + bx + c = 0. Enter coefficients a, b, and c to find the roots.

faq.a: x = (-b ± √(b²-4ac)) / 2a. It gives the solutions (roots) of any quadratic equation ax²+bx+c=0.

faq.q: What does the discriminant tell us?
faq.a: The discriminant (b²-4ac) determines the nature of roots: positive = 2 real roots, zero = 1 repeated root, negative = 2 complex roots.
